entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level retention for users, sessions, and autonomous agents. It utilizes hybrid retrieval and temporal reasoning to rank memories by relevant dates and states.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An open-source context database that uses a filesystem-based directory paradigm and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading. This approach significantly reduces token usage while improving task-completion rates via recursive directory retrieval.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring sub-300ms recall and byte-level deduplication for prompt-cache discounts. It automates user profile maintenance and includes native connectors for Notion, GitHub, and Google Drive.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent that synthesizes cited answers with explicit gap analysis of missing information. It features a self-wiring knowledge graph for complex entity traversal and MCP support for tools like Cursor and Claude Code.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system implementing retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge. It offers a simple LLM wrapper for automatic storage and supports enterprise backends like PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A memory provider that treats context as a git repository, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of markdown context trees. It delivers sub-100ms retrieval across a 5-tier system and includes built-in tools for code execution.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions rather than relying on simple vector matching. It maintains evolving representations of users and projects in prompt-ready formats compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving context harness that uses five specialized agent roles to generate structured playbooks. It enables persistent knowledge transfer through CLI-first skill exports for Hermes Agent.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency, SQLite-backed memory system delivering sub-millisecond performance. It combines vector similarity, FTS5, and importance scoring in an MCP-ready integration.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical summary DAG for depth-aware compaction. It preserves raw messages in SQLite and provides dedicated tools like lcm_grep for precise retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and any OpenAI-compatible LLM via a Docker-based setup.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that operates without API keys or cloud dependencies. It employs a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A portable identity and memory layer that ensures state continuity across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw. It uses SQLite and markdown for local data ownership and hybrid graph-vector retrieval.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format. It implements ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information without API costs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits and reduces prefill latency through prefix reordering and deduplication. It is designed to accelerate inference across SGLang, vLLM, and the Hermes ecosystem.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to provide bounded evidence packing that reduces prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system featuring semantic search and ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories and URLs, using edge tags to create navigable relationships with inverse links.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall via why_retrieved tags. It tracks contradictions and handles canonicalization without requiring a separate server or GPU.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses a local-first SQLite-vec storage and an MCP-native server for seamless integration with coding agents.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt overhead by using BM25-based ranking to select only the most relevant tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to monitor token savings across hybrid and Anthropic Tool Search modes.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database. It employs importance-weighted recall to surface high-priority memories and supports GLiNER2 for entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of memories without leaving persistent summaries. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log to track every memory operation for transparency.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first design and prototyping system that integrates directly with Hermes Agent and 15 other coding-agent CLIs. It provides 137 composable skills and 150 design systems with support for multiple export formats including HTML, PDF, and PPTX.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A library of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It follows the agentskills.io standard to enable sub-second discovery and execution across 26 security domains.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Enables the generation of .drawio XML diagrams from natural language descriptions with six specialized presets for architecture, UML, and deep learning. It utilizes visual feedback loops to self-check and automatically fix layout issues.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: An auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit-in-place modes. It uses a 109-entry word replacement table and 47 pattern categories to ensure content compatibility with Hermes and other AI editors.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across different agents and teams while integrating natively with Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library offering modular frameworks for UX design, architecture, marketing, and product strategy. These skills are compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents via a marketplace or npx CLI.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skills. It generates Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, manageable via dedicated slash commands.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe agentskills.io support for Pydantic AI, utilizing progressive disclosure to reduce token consumption. It supports both programmatic Python definitions and filesystem-based skill loading.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A suite of tools for extracting transcripts, captions, and timestamps from YouTube without relying on yt-dlp. It enables Hermes Agent and other compatible tools to perform video searches, channel browsing, and playlist extraction.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and one-click installation of skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with crypto/Stripe payments and an agent-to-agent trust scoring system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Teaches Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem solving. It identifies structural conservation laws in codebases and generates constraint reports to highlight analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that manages shared state locally in .maestro/ without needing a database. It decomposes complex specifications into execution plans and uses JSON handoff envelopes for seamless cross-session transitions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves prose, diagrams, and source code into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the original literate document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Implements cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories to help agents remember previous work. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting training data and fine-tuning replacement models.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Standardized skills for interacting with the Chainlink oracle network, including CCIP, Data Feeds, and VRF. It supports local simulations and CRE onboarding, compatible with Vercel's open skills ecosystem CLI.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures traceability by linking instructions to specific source file line numbers and uses version-pinning to prevent instruction rot.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation, providing standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry for reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification, featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI. It supports recursive dependency resolution and monetization via Stripe and the TON blockchain.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Provides autonomous execution for long-running, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It includes built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery during complex task execution.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to reduce token usage by up to 86%. It employs a socket-based daemon for persistent connections and supports hot-reloading configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: Integrates Hermes Agent with Nextcloud to manage files, notes, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It utilizes SKILL.md files for progressive disclosure and integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based skill loading.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages for various AI coding agents and CLIs, including Hermes. It features automated daily updates to ensure users always have access to the most recent tool versions within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers specialized scripts and configuration guides for running agent CLIs on ARM64 Android devices via Termux. It integrates with Termux:API and Termux:GUI, allowing Hermes and other OpenClaw agents to interact directly with smartphone hardware.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop solution provides a GUI-driven Hermes experience with 100 built-in tools and local model support via LM Studio. It functions as a portable automation engine, allowing users to build custom workflow pipelines without requiring Docker or complex installations.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: Designed for mobile privacy and cost-efficiency, this project features a one-line Termux installer for running Hermes Agent locally on Android. It supports over 200 models, including local execution via Ollama and remote access through major API providers.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart enables cloud-native deployment of Hermes Agent on Kubernetes with a focus on state safety. It enforces single-writer storage patterns and offers configurable gateway modes, making it suitable for operator-ready production workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw provides a high-security sandbox for Hermes Agent using kernel-level enforcement to restrict network, filesystem, and process access. It allows users to apply dynamic security policies while integrating the agent with IDEs, MCP servers, and messaging platforms.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent in a sandboxed environment. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of Hermes using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, including systemd service configuration and secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between Nix-managed reproducibility and interactive CLI usage for a hybrid deployment model.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Focused on production-ready headless operation, this tool configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically designed for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It features workspace isolation, task scheduling, and inter-agent delegation, providing an enterprise-grade management layer for complex agent hierarchies.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack optimized for Portainer, featuring a ttyd web terminal for remote browser-based access. It simplifies management through a Git Repository stack and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration stack provides a zero-dependency SQLite environment for dispatching tasks across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen workflows. It distinguishes itself with the Aegis review system, which implements quality gates to monitor agent spend and task accuracy.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A versatile control surface that manages parallel sessions for Hermes, Claude Code, and other agents using tmux for persistent background execution. It offers a mobile-first web dashboard and TUI, utilizing Docker or Podman to ensure all agent activities remain sandboxed.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: This template introduces a sidecar control plane focused on agent governance and documentation for scaling from single VPS instances to specialist teams. It utilizes a structured task bus pattern to facilitate formal delegation between orchestrators and sub-agents.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: Minions provides a centralized mission control interface featuring a Kanban board for tracking autonomous task statuses and review queues. It emphasizes human-in-the-loop verification by live-streaming agent reasoning and tool execution for real-time supervision.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous swarms across 23+ providers, including Ollama and Hermes, with built-in heartbeats and scheduling. It features a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism and durable memory to evolve agent capabilities over time.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: Designed for infrastructure-heavy workflows, this level-based architecture scales Hermes from a single agent to automated teams capable of VPS provisioning and security auditing. It leverages a task bus pattern to manage handoffs and specialized skill sets for system administration.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols for tool handling. The platform integrates a real-time Web UI and Hermes Kanban to monitor the entire lifecycle of agent dialogues and task progress.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project deploys a massive cohort of 17 specialized agents dedicated to distinct phases like research, planning, and infrastructure. It enforces strict quality gates through mandatory reviewer and tester agents to ensure code integrity across customizable pipelines.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization framework that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code of Hermes agents. It tracks performance frontiers by orchestrating TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, utilizing candidate mutation to improve agent efficiency.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes and Paperclip, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine agents per side and visualizes their code execution and scoreboard metrics on a real-time dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits on all delegated sub-tasks.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: This local indexing tool optimizes Hermes Agent performance by mapping code symbols into a knowledge graph, reducing tool calls by approximately 58%. It operates entirely without external dependencies, allowing agents to navigate complex repositories while significantly lowering token consumption.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust TUI designed to track and visualize token usage across multiple AI coding clients, including Hermes and Claude Code. It aggregates data from local databases and JSONL files to provide developers with clear cost and usage insights.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: This local trace viewer enables developers to inspect API traffic, prompts, and tool calls generated by Hermes Agent and other coding assistants. It reconstructs streaming responses for debugging and can generate portable HTML artifacts for sharing or archiving session traces.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A customization utility for the Hermes terminal interface that allows users to modify banner colors, ASCII art, and UI labels. It supports a library of themed presets and partial YAML configurations that inherit from default settings for easy personalization.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that orchestrates 23 autonomous skills for building, shipping, and monitoring applications. It automates high-level tasks like GitHub issue triage and security reviews while integrating directly with platforms like Vercel and Supabase.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for OAuth or external processes. It includes specialized MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training within the native environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser built for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a human-in-the-loop interface for action rollbacks and approvals, alongside durable session management for structured agent checkpoints.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with project-local memories and tools. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between global and scoped configurations to maintain context-aware custom instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: This setup utilizes RTK and Caveman templating to achieve up to 99% token savings through optimized CLI workflows. It comes pre-configured with over 40 skills for finance and DevOps, featuring an automated installer for macOS and Linux.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that uses deterministic structural detectors to scan agent configurations and system prompts for failure modes without making LLM calls. It integrates into CI/CD pipelines to provide HERM v1.1 scoring and fast, cost-free validation of agent logic.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A Docker-based deployment stack that configures Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local setup. It includes 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, automating the routing for free models and local GPU inference.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A specialized migration utility that transfers agent personas, memories, and workspace files from OpenClaw to the Hermes Agent format. It maps complex model configurations to Hermes YAML and handles the secure transfer of local credentials.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform desktop manager built with Tauri 2 that provides a unified interface for Hermes Agent alongside other major CLI agents like Claude Code and Gemini. It simplifies the lifecycle of multiple AI tools by offering a single, high-performance application for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: This local open-source 'cowork' app provides a unified GUI for over 13 CLI agents, including Hermes Agent, with built-in web search and MCP tool integration. It stands out for its ability to automatically generate and edit professional documents like Morph PPTs, Word files, and Excel sheets.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight, zero-build web interface that brings full Hermes CLI parity to browsers and mobile devices using only Python and vanilla JavaScript. It features a functional three-panel layout that includes workspace file browsing and inline previews for a seamless remote experience.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A comprehensive desktop companion designed to guide users through the installation and configuration of local or remote Hermes backends. It offers visual management for personas, memory, and skills, while supporting an expansive 16 messaging gateways for broad connectivity.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: This dashboard focuses on multi-platform deployment, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes Agent across Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Matrix. It includes robust usage analytics for tracking token consumption and costs alongside real-time session management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that introduces 'Swarm Mode' for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents within a single interface. It provides a unified environment for chat, terminal access, and memory editing without requiring forks of the vanilla Hermes Agent.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A specialized Mac workspace that connects to Hermes hosts via SSH to provide a native terminal and session experience. It includes productivity-focused views like Kanban boards and file explorers to manage agentic workflows directly from macOS.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted operational control plane that features a three-panel Kanban pipeline for real-time swarm monitoring. It is particularly useful for power users needing deep oversight of MCP servers, system metrics, and detailed cost projections.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A privacy-focused macOS session browser that indexes local AI agent histories without telemetry or remote logging. Its 'Agent Cockpit' allows for live monitoring of active iTerm2 sessions, making it an ideal tool for auditing and searching past agent interactions.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: Designed for Orgo cloud computers, this macOS workspace offers one-click automated installation of Hermes Agent on fresh cloud VMs. It uniquely integrates WebRTC voice mode, enabling real-time vocal interaction with the agent via Orgo MCP.
entries.liftaris/herm: An operator-focused TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI that emphasizes efficiency through profile switching and session management. It features a native Eikon Marketplace, allowing users to easily search for and install shared agent extensions.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed to run as a Telegram Mini App, offering secure HMAC-SHA256 authentication. It supports advanced mobile features like file attachments with automated vision analysis and OCR capabilities for on-the-go agent management.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-optimized desktop application built with Tauri and Rust that provides a localized interface for the Chinese Hermes community. It streamlines the management of the Hermes kernel, including automated updates and integrated UI for MCP tools and memory.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install, 'no-admin' Windows GUI that bundles over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI. It is designed for portability and ease of use, featuring a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers and Python handlers without Docker.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic, single-file React application that uses a lightweight Python proxy to provide a modern web interface. It excels at real-time log streaming and live workspace browsing, offering a sleek way to manage skills and cron jobs.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This dashboard serves as a monitoring and configuration hub, providing a real-time activity feed with cost tracking and model distribution data. It allows for read-only inspection of agent skill sources and full-text search across all conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that prioritizes security and organization through isolated profile management for separate API keys and memory. It features a chat-first design with real-time SSE streaming and interactive tool approval cards for human-in-the-loop workflows.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that orchestrates collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents. It includes a visual 'Skill Store' with over 50 curated one-click installations, making it easy to expand the agent's capabilities through a native interface.
